summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a tight Spring‑89 show, highlighted by a smoking Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain and stellar Bird Song while The Saint falls flat.
review: The performance captures one of the hottest nights of the spring‑1989 tour – tight playing, soaring vocals from Bob and Jerry, Brent at his peak and Phil’s bass thundering through the Mecca.  Recording quality is crystal clear and well balanced, though a few reviewers note The Saint as a weak spot and an absent Good Lovin’ encore.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and repeated praise for both the musical performance and the clear soundboard source drive a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix with superb instrument separation; no reported drop‑outs or muffiness. Reviewers repeatedly call the audio "superb" and "awesome".

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, well‑played set with strong first‑set highlights and a seamless flow into a high‑energy second set; overall the show is described as one of the tightest and hottest of 1989.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "all there" with brilliant guitar tone and powerful vocals, no noodling.
band_member_comments.Phil: "Bombed the Mecca" – bass was massive and driving throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Belting lyrics with crunchy, delicious guitar fills; strong vocal harmonies with Jerry.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland praised as being at his absolute best, lifting the entire band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers kept a solid beat while remaining creative and adventurous.
